core_thesis: Husserl's thesis is that philosophy must return to the things themselves by suspending the natural attitude and describing how objectivity is constituted in intentional acts ([Ideas I §§31-32](cite://husserl/ideas-i/31-32); [Cartesian Meditations §13](cite://husserl/cartesian-meditations/13)). Consciousness is always consciousness of something, and the object is given through horizons, profiles, and syntheses rather than as a bare thing outside appearance. Transcendental reduction thus does not abolish the world; it discloses the structures by which the world has sense for consciousness.

cross_engagements.passage: Cross-engagement: Abhinavagupta presses the same selfhood structure axis in the phenomenological register, but with the value `relational-self` rather than `transcendental-ego` in [primary text](cite://abhinavagupta/tantraloka/1.16). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Adi Sankara presses the same finite cognition axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `adhyasa-or-superimposition` rather than `intentional-constitution` in [primary text](cite://sankara/brahma-sutra-bhasya/1.1.1). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Akalanka presses the same epistemic authority axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `plural-pramana-realism` rather than `phenomenological-reduction` in [primary text](cite://umasvati/tattvartha-sutra/1.34-35). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Akshapada Gautama presses the same method axis in the methodological register, but with the value `formal-proof-or-inference` rather than `phenomenological-reduction` in [primary text](cite://aksapada/nyaya-sutra/1.1.1). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Baladeva Vidyabhushana presses the same perspectivism axis in the epistemological register, but with the value `partial-perspectives-ranked` rather than `standpoint-conditioned-realism` in [primary text](cite://badarayana/brahma-sutra/1.1.1). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Anandabodha presses the same practice-path axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `knowledge-discipline` rather than `reduction-or-attentive-description` in [primary text](cite://sarvajnatman/samksepa-sariraka/1.319-352). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Anantakrishna Sastri presses the same soteric end axis in the soteriological register, but with the value `identity-with-ground` rather than `not-soteriological` in [primary text](cite://sankara/brahma-sutra-bhasya/2.1.14). The disagreement is structural, not verbal. Both texts are answering the same pressure-point; they are not locating the pressure in the same way.

Cross-engagement: Bannanje Govindacharya touches the same individuation status axis, but not in the same register. This section works it as `transcendental-pole` in the phenomenological register; Bannanje Govindacharya approaches it through the metaphysical register in [primary text](cite://madhva/tattva-sankhyana/1-4). The point of contact is genuine, but the comparison only holds once the register-shift is kept visible.
